memories.content:
- User request: drills to reduce anxiety for motorway driving and parallel parking.
- Motorway driving: 
  - Mindset/prep: study typical entry/exit ramps and signs; plan maneuvers early (mirror → signal → manoeuvre) mentally before practice.
  - Progression: (1) dual carriageway practice on quiet road; (2) short slip-road entries/exits off-peak; (3) short mainline stints with one or two junctions; (4) longer, busier times, night/rain when comfortable.
  - Specific drills: slip-road merge (5–8 reps), lane-change rehearsal (10 controlled changes per session), overtaking/merge gap judgment (with instructor
- The data presents a 12-week plan to blend clutch

The recommended minimum is about 40 hours of total driving experience before sitting the practical exam; with ~10 hours already, about 30 more hours are suggested. The 40 hours typically break down into 8–12 hours with a professional instructor and 20–30 hours of supervised family practice. These are guidelines, not legal requirements, and the exact split depends on practice effectiveness. Key factors beyond hours include consistency of performance, quality and variety of practice, and frequent short sessions. Read

- Week 1 — Foundations (control & observation)
  - Maneuvers/skills: clutch bite point, smooth take-offs/stops, simple straight reversing, mirror checks.
  - Session focus: heavy clutch/drivetrain control drills + short low-speed drives.
  - Who: instructor preferred for the first lesson; dad for repetition.
  - Drills/reps: bite-point moves in 10–20 cm steps (15–30 reps), 10 controlled starts/stops, 5 short reversing runs.
  - Readiness goal: move off smoothly without stalling 9/10 times; consistent basic mirror use.

- Week 2 — Consolidate clutch & low-speed manoeuvring
  - Maneuvers/skills: clutch control under turns, low-speed steering, stall recovery.
  - Session focus: combine clutch drills with short roadway exposure (quiet streets).
  - Who: instructor (2nd lesson) then dad.
  - Drills/reps: slow slalom and tight turns (8–12 reps), controlled stop/start (10–15 reps), 6–8 stall recoveries.
  - Readiness goal: comfortable with controlled turns and calm stall recovery.

- Week 3 — Hill starts, controlled

- The user asked for simple, step-by-step cues or drills to build confidence and muscle memory for parallel parking on busy streets.
- The response provides a general step-by-step manoeuvre (five steps) with reference points and distances: prepare/position 50–70 cm from the lead car; reverse with full lock toward the curb; straighten wheels after a reference point clears the rear bumper; turn away to tuck in and align parallel to the curb with 20–30 cm from the curb; pull forward to center if needed.
- It includes key in-drive cue phrases: “Position — rear even,” “Reverse — full curb,” “Straighten — clear front,” “Turn away — tuck,” “Adjust — centre.”
- It outlines progression drills
